Wednesday, Jun 10 · 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM PDT

The Academy

2166 Market Street, · San Francisco, CA

San Francisco dbt Meetup

Hosted by Karen H.

San Francisco dbt Meetup
4.8
46 reviews
Details

What are dbt meetups? dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🤝Organizer: Karen Hsieh (winner of the dbt Community Award, and longtime organizer of the Taipei dbt Meetup)
🏠Venue: 2166 Market Street, San Francisco, CA 94114
🍕Catering: Light bites and refreshments

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

## Agenda

5:00–5:30 PM: Mingling with refreshments
5:30–5:40 PM: Opening
5:40–8:00 PM: Lightlingten Talks + More mingling
7:30–8:00 PM: Clean up while we are still chatting

## Lighten Talks

Vince Faller on dbt-wizard — an opinionated pattern for metadata checks in dbt. He's curious what people think about the approach. Come with takes.
Hamzah Chaudhary demoing Lightdash Data Apps: type a prompt, get a governed data app backed by your semantic layer. Beta just opened in May.
Luca Martial demoing ktx by Kaelio (YC X25): open-source context layer so AI agents can query your data accurately —built on top of dbt.
Karen Hsieh talking through an agentic workflow she uses to keep up with data requests.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/ and join the #local-sf channel.
----------------------------------
dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 40,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Read more

events.page_title: dbt Global Circuit Series: San Francisco, Thu, Jun 12, 2025, 3:00 PM | Meetup
events.raw_text:
Skip to content

Thu, Jun 12, 2025 · 3:00 PM to 5:00 PM PDT

Trellis Coworking, Events, Cafe & Bar

981 Mission St · San Francisco, CA

dbt Global Circuit Series: San Francisco

Hosted by Ani V. and Drew B.

San Francisco dbt Meetup
4.8
46 reviews
Details

In this meetup, we’ll dive into the latest tools and ideas we’ve been shipping — what they unlock, how they fit together, and why we built them the way we did. 🚶 Walk on over after Data + AI Summit (11 minute walk), which wraps up at 2:30pm!

This meetup is designed for builders, thinkers, and tinkerers — folks who want to roll up their sleeves (figuratively), learn from each other, and imagine what’s next for the analytics engineering stack.

Whether you’re curious about what’s new, want to see how others are approaching similar problems, or just want to ask hard questions — you’re in the right place.

🤝Organizer: dbt Labs Community Team (Emcee: Drew Banin)
🏠Venue: Trellis Coworking, Events, Cafe & Bar @ 981 Mission St (11 minute walk from Moscone Center)
🍕Catering: open bar and bites

📝Agenda

3:00 | Check in & Mingling
3:30 | Rethinking the dbt Semantic Layer: 3 Underrated Use Cases by Juan Manuel Perafan (Head Coach, Nimbus Academy @ Nimbus Intelligence) + Please hold your questions for networking
The dbt Semantic Layer is often pitched as a way to standardize metrics for BI tools, but that’s just the surface. In this talk, we’ll explore three lesser-known, practical ways to use it that go beyond reporting.
3:45 | “For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs” by Ani Venkateshwaran (Software Engineer @ dbt Labs, and ex-SDF Labs) + Live Q&A
4:00 - 5:00 | Reception

EVENT DETAILS:

Come on over after Data + AI Summit, which wraps up at 2:30pm! If you didn't make it to Data + AI Summit, even better. Come meet folks from the conference and learn about your latest tools.
Please make sure you RSVP so we order the right amount of food and drinks!
Please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝 Make sure to join the #local-sf channel in dbt Slack

***

Since 2016, dbt Labs has been on a mission to help data practitioners create and disseminate organizational knowledge. dbt is the standard for AI-ready structured data. Powered by the dbt Fusion engine, it unlocks the performance, context, and trust that organizations need to scale analytics in the era of AI. Globally, more than 60,000 data teams use dbt, including those at Siemens, Roche and Condé Nast.

Learn more at getdbt.com, and follow dbt Labs on LinkedIn, X, Instagram, and YouTube.

Karen H.

426 days ago

·
Host

Hey,

Whether you joined this meetup or not, I’d love to share the recording with you.

I’m the winner of the dbt Community Award and longtime organizer of the Taipei dbt Meetup. Since I was traveling to SF, I hosted this meetup — and we’re sharing the recording on the dbt-local-taipei YouTube channel to support dbt communities everywhere. 🌏

In her talk, “dbt’s Semantic Layer: A Practical Guide from a Looker Convert,” Dori explores:

Why semantic layers often fail in practice
Common misconceptions
How to rethink semantics by focusing on people and workflows — not just tools

🎥 Watch the recording: https://youtu.be/4n_RStWpzTQ
Hope you enjoy it! Let me know what you think. 🙌

Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event

99 attendees from 54 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

San Francisco dbt Meetup
4.8
46 reviews
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

events.raw_text:

Mon, Jul 15, 2024 · 5:30 PM to 8:00 PM PDT

650 California St suite 1501

650 California St suite 1501 · San Francisco, CA

‘dbt Labs on dbt’ Meetup: San Francisco
D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

San Francisco dbt Meetup
4.8
46 reviews
Details

🤝Organizer: dbt Labs
🏠Venue Host: dbt Labs (650 California Street Suite 1501)
🍕Catering: light bites and refreshments

🗣️Description: In the dbt Labs on dbt series, you’ll get a behind-the-scenes look at how dbt Labs uses our own product. You’ll learn how dbt Labs thinks about the role of data, how data developers collaborate with business leaders, and the technical decisions we’ve made in our own dbt project.
Title/Topic: "Analytics Engineer 🤝 Data Analyst -- brought to you by dbt Cloud." In this particular session, Paige Berry (Sr. Data Analyst, dbt Labs) and Lauren Benezra (Senior Analytics Engineer, dbt Labs) will discuss how they work on projects together, how they collaborate, and how they build solutions from end-to-end.

🎤Speaker bios:
Paige Berry is a Senior Data Analyst at dbt Labs, where she loves being a “data investigative journalist” (e.g. finding “the scoop” in the data), and making custom emojis for the company Slack. She’s been working with data for two decades, getting her start as a programmer/analyst in higher ed in 2004 before diving into the world of SaaS tech companies in 2018. Discovering analytics engineering and dbt two years later was a game changer. Outside of work, she enjoys making 1:24 scale miniature houses from kits, exploring the natural beauty of the Pacific Northwest, researching who to add to/drop from her fantasy hockey and football team lineups, or reading an anthology of science fiction short stories with one of her several cats snoozing on her lap.

Lauren Benezra is a Senior Analytics Engineer at dbt Labs, and a full-time cat lady. Before discovering analytics engineering, she earned a Master’s in Applied Math, modeling biological systems and cat population dynamics. Later, she became an automation engineer, building all-things-code for data systems and laboratory robotics in the biotech world, where she discovered her love of organizing the unorganized, and the world of dbt. Beyond her day job, trains Jiu-Jitsu, socializes her feral foster cats, and snuggles with her own three cats.

📝Agenda
5:30-6:00 Checkin
6:00-6:30 Presentation
6:30-6:40 Q&A
6:40-8:00 Networking

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-sf channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
----------------------------------

Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

444 Castro St suite 1000 · Mountain View, CA

How to find us
Mountain View dbt Meetup (in-person)
D

Hosted by dbt L. and 2 others

dbt L. is a Super Organizer

San Francisco dbt Meetup
4.8
46 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🤝Organizer: 4 Mile Analytics
🏠Venue Host: Thoughtspot
🍕Catering: Taco Bar

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

📝Agenda

5:30 - 6:00 | Check in/Registration (30 min)
6:00 - 6:15 | Welcome Remarks by 4 Mile Analytics (5 min)
6:45 - 6:45 | Managing batch and real-time streaming data transformation together with dbt: An Open Source Adapter
Rachel Pedreschi - Head of Technical Services, Decodable
Eric Sammer - Founder, Head of Product, Decodable
6:45 - 7:00 | Closing Remarks by 4 Mile Analytics
7:00 - 8:00 | Reception and networking

🗣️Presentation topic1: "Managing batch and real-time streaming data transformation together with dbt: an open source adapter"

Speakers: Rachel Pedreschi is the Head of Technical Services at Decodable. Eric Sammer is the Founder, Head of Product at Decodable

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/

🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-sf channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
----------------------------------
dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.
**Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/**

Details

In case you missed it, dbt Labs is hosting Coalesce 2022 from October 17 - 21. It is the annual conference dedicated to advancing the practice of analytics engineering. Coalesce offers data practitioners an opportunity to deepen their knowledge and network. You can choose your own adventure including in-person, online, and hybrid experiences.

Coalesce Online (FREE) | October 17-21, 2022. The online experience that you know and love. Connect with data practitioners around the globe in sessions designed for North America, APAC, EMEA and LATAM regions. Catch live stream sessions from Coalesce New Orleans, interact with the community and speakers via Slack, and attend exclusively virtual sessions.
Coalesce New Orleans | October 17-21, 2022 | Located at the Hyatt Regency, New Orleans. Our first time together, in person! Attend a presentation, workshop, or panel discussion. Participate in activities, a hackathon, intimate discussions, and social activities. Meet community members and partners while exploring the history and culture of New Orleans.
Coalesce Online + | London, 20 October 2022 | Sydney, 21 October 2022. A one-day event taking place in London and Sydney, complementing Coalesce Online. This will include tech talks, hosted discussions, an Executive keynote and a post-event networking session.

⚠️Please note that Coalesce 2022 won't be hosted on Meetup.com.
✅REGISTER + SIGN UP for sessions here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com
📅Review the schedule here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com/schedule
⭐As a member of the dbt Meetup Network, please use your discount code MEETUP15 for Premium and GA tickets.

events.raw_text:

Mon, Apr 18, 2022 · 6:00 PM to 7:00 PM CEST

Online event

This event has passed

Using Open Source Delta Lake & dbt in ML/Data Pipelines (online demo + webinar)
D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

San Francisco dbt Meetup
4.8
46 reviews
Details

We thought you might enjoy a talk on "How to efficiently manage the Data Engineer | Data Scientist handoff with dbt, Redshift, and Sagemaker" by Matt Winkler at the Data Science on AWS event. More details below:

RSVP Webinar: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/webinarkubeflow-tensorflow-tfx-pytorch-gpu-spark-ml-amazonsagemaker-tickets-45852865154

Talk #0: Introductions and Meetup Announcements By Chris Fregly and Antje Barth

Talk #1: Using Delta Lake with Amazon SageMaker
Speaker: Paul Hargis and Vedant Jain, Senior Solution Architects, AI and Machine Learning @ AWS

Talk #2: How to efficiently manage the Data Engineer | Data Scientist handoff with dbt, Redshift, and Sagemaker by Matt Winkler from *dbt Labs*
Description: dbt (data build tool) is an open source transformation framework which enables testing, documentation, and version control on top of data pipelines. In this talk, we’ll discuss how dbt can be used to manage feature engineering pipelines for Machine Learning, and the handoff points between dbt and models managed in AWS Sagemaker.
Speaker Bio: Hi, I’m Matt! I’m an ex- data scientist who chose to embrace the simplicity of SQL in managing and testing data pipelines with dbt. I’ve previously worked as a hands-on ML practitioner, and consulted with Fortune 500 clients to build and maintain ML Ops pipelines using (mostly) AWS Sagemaker. I live in the Denver area, and you can say hello on dbt Slack or on LinkedIn.

https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/about/viewpoint/
https://github.com/dbt-labs/dbt-core
https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/available-adapters

RSVP Webinar: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/webinarkubeflow-tensorflow-tfx-pytorch-gpu-spark-ml-amazonsagemaker-tickets-45852865154
Zoom link: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/82308186562

# Related Links

O'Reilly Book: https://www.amazon.com/dp/1492079391/
Website: https://datascienceonaws.com
Meetup: https://meetup.datascienceonaws.com
GitHub Repo: https://github.com/data-science-on-aws/
YouTube: https://youtube.datascienceonaws.com
Slideshare: https://slideshare.datascienceonaws.com

Fri, Sep 17, 2021 · 12:00 AM to 1:30 AM CEST

Online event

This event has passed

San Francisco dbt Meetup - ONLINE September Edition

Hosted by Rosie C.

San Francisco dbt Meetup
4.8
46 reviews
Details

✨ What to expect at this meetup ✨

Transformation Strategy: tETLTt

When and where is it best to transform data? Is there still a place for transformations before loading into the DWH? Should metric aggregations occur within the DWH and not the BI platform of choice? Should we ever consider transforming within the transactional database itself?

This session will be an open discussion of where transformation could and should occur, what use case makes the most sense with each type of transformation, and some interesting edge cases where data is transformed in unusual ways. Join us to learn how to incorporate novel transformation strategies into your tETLTt flow!

This short discussion will be followed by 3 break out sessions that will cover a variety of topics selected by registrants.

Speakers / Meetup organizers:
Callum McCann - Solutions Architect
Anmol Singh - Application Engineer
Joel McKelvey - VP, Product Marketing

Agenda:

General Discussion and Q&A on Transformation Strategy
3 Breakout Sessions running concurrently
Review of breakout sessions
Close and Q&A

** Vote for the breakout session topic you want to cover!
https://forms.gle/rUxsramisWdJuAwr7

dbt Meetups are gatherings dedicated to helping you own your analytics engineering workflow. Events will focus on the impact of dbt (data build tool) in the data community and professional development tools for analytics engineers.

Expect big-picture conversations related to the data stacks, data operations, data teams, modeling, testing, dashboards, etc.

If you work with data, this group is for you! We welcome data analysts, scientists, engineers, architects, and more!!

Join the conversation in the #local-sf channel in dbt Slack! This is where the chat will take place during meetups.

✨ What to expect at this meetup ✨

Come hear from leaders in the data industry!

We'll offer advice to data teams on how they can improve their work and make their companies more data-driven.

We'll touch on what changes are happening in the industry and the evolution of the modern data stack.

We'll be joined by:

Julia Schottenstein, Product Manager, Fishtown Analytics
Sameer Al-Sakran, CEO, Metabase
Boris Jabes, CEO, Census
Gleb Mezhanskiy, CEO & Co-Founder, Datafold

The one and only, Mila Page (Developer Relations Advocate at Fishtown Analytics) will moderate the discussion.

Thu, Jul 30, 2020 · 8:00 PM to 9:00 PM CEST

Online event

This event has passed

San Francisco dbt Meetup Online

Hosted by Claire C.

San Francisco dbt Meetup
4.8
46 reviews
Details

Welcome to the first online-only San Francisco Meetup! We are disappointed we can't meet in person, but are still looking forward to seeing your friendly faces on Zoom.

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members’ experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #events-sf channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------------

Agenda ✅

11:00 am - 11:05 am: Welcome Remarks & Attendee Intros
11:05 am - 11:20 am: Responding in an emergency with data, DataSF, City & County of San Francisco
11:20 am - 11:30 am: DataSF, City & County of San Francisco Q&A
11:30 am - 11:45 am: Improving data reliability using tests in dbt, Zoox
11:45 am - 11:55 am: Zoox Q&A
11:55 am - 12:00 pm: Closing Remarks

----------------------------------------

Speaker & Presentation Details:

Responding in an emergency with data 🎤
Tania Jogesh, Data Scientist, DataSF, City & County of San Francisco
Blake Valenta, Data Analyst & Analytics Strategist, DataSF, City & County of San Francisco
Jason Lally, Chief Data Officer, DataSF, City & County of San Francisco

Discover how DataSF responded to the Covid-19 emergency and provided a 360-degree view of PPE (personal protective equipment) inventory. Leveraging dbt, agile processes and empathy, DataSF brought together different data sources in a rapidly changing environment while minimizing disruption to those responding to the crisis.

Improving data reliability using tests in dbt 🎤
Evgeny Rubtsov, Data Engineer, Zoox

Trust is one of the most important assets that a data team has, and nothing undermines trust as much as repeated issues with data accuracy. Learn how Zoox ensures data integrity with dbt: testing at runtime (vs. CI/CD), using schema tests so that issues with data sources are not contaminating public models, and using dbt snapshots to catch the cases when data sources alter historic data.

----------------------------------------

dbt is a command line tool that speaks the preferred language of data analysts everywhere—SQL. With dbt, analysts take ownership of the entire analytics engineering workflow, from writing data transformation code to deployment and documentation.

events.raw_text:

Tue, Nov 5, 2019 · 6:00 PM to 8:30 PM PST

Envoy

410 Townsend St 4th floor · San Francisco, CA

dbt Meetup at Envoy

Hosted by Claire C.

San Francisco dbt Meetup
4.8
46 reviews
Details

This event is hosted by Envoy (www.envoy.com) and open to all folks working with data—while we'll predominantly focus on people's experience with dbt, we're also very interested in broader topics related to analytics engineering, such as data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

Doors open at 6pm, for a 6:30 start! We'll bring the food and drinks, just make sure you have your ID to sign in!

----------------------------------------
SPEAKER LINEUP

Building trust in your data
Andrea Koptiz, Data Analyst
Envoy
If there is one data set that you want to be completely error-free, it’s financial data. Learn how Envoy used human feedback along with a rigorous testing process to debug financial models. Their experience is a lesson in both writing great data tests and also working incredibly well with cross-functional teams.

How to survive as a data team of one
Martin Guindon, Asst. Director, BI
Absolunet.com
Modern data tools have made the one-person analytics team not only possible, but incredibly impactful. Still, the role requires a unique mindset. This talk will cover a range of lessons learned–from choosing the right tooling to working effectively with non-data team members.

Modeling pricing configuration data
Tyler Pugliese
Fastly
Salesforce CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote) allows companies to provide accurate pricing even with a myriad of product configuration scenarios. This valuable intelligence on the sales pipeline was one of Fastly’s newest and most requested data sets. Learn how the data team used dbt to turn raw, isolated CPQ data into a polished dataset, integrating the model into Fastly’s growing collection of sales data models.

Glue work in analytics
Caitlin Moorman
Yerdle Recommerce
We all want to do work that helps our teams be more effective and our organizations be more successful. But sometimes, that work isn't in our job description, and isn't properly valued within the organization. When work is valuable but not valued, it becomes glue work. How do you get un-stuck and make sure your work is recognized and rewarded?

----------------------------------------
dbt is a command line tool that speaks the preferred language of data analysts everywhere—SQL. With dbt, analysts take ownership of the entire analytics engineering workflow, from writing data transformation code to deployment and documentation.
Read more

Wed, Jun 12, 2019 · 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM PDT

1301 Sansome St

1301 Sansome St · San Francisco, CA

dbt Meetup at Grove Collaborative

Hosted by Claire C.

San Francisco dbt Meetup
4.8
46 reviews
Details

This event is hosted by Grove Collaborative (www.grove.co) and open to all folks working with data—while we'll predominantly focus on people's experience with dbt, we're also very interested in broader topics that apply to modern data teams, such as data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

With dbt (www.getdbt.com), data analysts and engineers can build analytics the way engineers build applications.

This meetup will feature three talks:
• Ameena Khan, Marketing Data Analyst at Grove Collaborative, will be sharing her experience implementing dbt to established data team and what she would do differently the next time.

• Siobhán K Cronin, Data Engineering Lead at Landed, will cover how her data engineering team of two provides clean, transformed data to end users.

• TJ Murphy, Head of Data at Stealth (Topic is still TBD)

Light food and drink will be provided.
